content: We can frame the Self-Refine framework as a practical instantiation of abstract LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where multiple divergent problem perspectives from self-reflection research can be inserted directly into Self-Refine’s iterative loop to break it out of local optimization traps caused by single-perspective self-feedback. This creates a zero-training hybrid self-improvement system that retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of no extra training data while addressing its hidden limitation of narrow, consistent self-feedback.
